title: Project & Task management API
description: The epic-scale breadth scenario. A multi-resource REST API with auth, cross-resource relations, payload validation, and pagination exercises genuine Epic decomposition across multiple feature slices — auth, project CRUD, task CRUD with filtering, and persistence. Its surface area forces both arms to plan a multi-wave delivery and exposes divergence in decomposition quality, auth design, and integration depth.

seed.title: Project & Task management API with auth
seed.prompt:
Build a JSON REST API in Node.js (ESM) for a project and task management system. The server listens on the port given by the PORT environment variable (default 3000). Implement the following surfaces:

**Auth**
POST /auth/register — accepts { username, password } (both non-empty strings), creates a user, returns 201 with { id, username }. Returns 400 for missing/empty fields and 409 if the username is already taken.
POST /auth/login — accepts { username, password }, returns 200 with { token } (a bearer token the client sends as Authorization: Bearer <token>). Returns 401 for unknown username or wrong password.

All endpoints below require a valid bearer token; respond 401 for missing or invalid tokens.

**Projects**
POST /projects — accepts { name } (non-empty string), creates a project owned by the authenticated user, returns 201 with the created project as JSON (id, name, ownerId, createdAt). Returns 400 for an empty name.
GET /projects — returns 200 with a JSON array of all projects visible to the authenticated user.
GET /projects/:id — returns 200 with the project JSON, or 404 if not found.
DELETE /projects/:id — deletes the project and all its tasks; returns 204, or 404 if not found.

**Tasks**
POST /projects/:projectId/tasks — accepts { title, assigneeId? } (title a non-empty string; assigneeId optional, must be a valid user id if provided), creates a task belonging to the project and returns 201 with the task JSON (id, title, projectId, assigneeId, createdAt, done: false). Returns 400 for invalid payload or unknown assigneeId, 404 if project not found.
GET /projects/:projectId/tasks — returns 200 with a paginated JSON response: { items: Task[], total: number, page: number, pageSize: number }. Accepts ?page=N (default 1) and ?pageSize=N (default 20, max 100). Returns 404 if project not found.
PATCH /projects/:projectId/tasks/:taskId — accepts { title?, done? } (partial update). Returns 200 with updated task, 404 if project or task not found.
DELETE /projects/:projectId/tasks/:taskId — deletes the task, returns 204, or 404 if not found.

**Persistence & restart**
All data must survive a server restart (use an embedded SQLite database or equivalent on-disk store — not in-memory).

Add an npm "start" script.
acceptance: POST /auth/register with valid credentials returns 201 with { id, username } and persists the user
POST /auth/register with a duplicate username returns 409
POST /auth/register with missing or empty fields returns 400
POST /auth/login with valid credentials returns 200 with a bearer token
POST /auth/login with an unknown username or wrong password returns 401
Requests to protected endpoints without a token return 401
Requests to protected endpoints with an invalid token return 401
POST /projects with a valid name returns 201 with the created project (id, name, ownerId, createdAt)
POST /projects with an empty name returns 400
GET /projects returns 200 with a JSON array containing projects created by the authenticated user
GET /projects/:id returns the matching project, and 404 for an unknown id
DELETE /projects/:id removes the project and all its tasks and returns 204; a subsequent GET returns 404
POST /projects/:projectId/tasks with a valid title returns 201 with the task (id, title, projectId, assigneeId, createdAt, done: false)
POST /projects/:projectId/tasks with an assigneeId for a valid user sets the assignee; an unknown assigneeId returns 400
POST /projects/:projectId/tasks with a missing or empty title returns 400
GET /projects/:projectId/tasks returns a paginated response { items, total, page, pageSize } respecting ?page and ?pageSize
PATCH /projects/:projectId/tasks/:taskId updates title and/or done and returns the updated task
DELETE /projects/:projectId/tasks/:taskId removes the task and returns 204; a subsequent GET of the task list no longer includes it
Data for users, projects, and tasks persists across a server restart
